perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “ Confidential Information ” means, with respect to either party, any and all information in written, representational, electronic, verbal or other form relating directly or indirectly to the present or potential business, operation or financial condition of or relating to the disclosing party (including, but not limited to, information identified as being proprietary and/or confidential or pertaining to, pricing, marketing plans or strategy, volumes, services rendered, customers and suppliers lists, financial or technical or service matters or data, employee/agent/ consultant/officer/director related personal or sensitive data and any information which might reasonably be presumed to be proprietary or confidential in nature) excluding any such information which (i) is known to the public (through no act or omission of the receiving party in violation of this Agreement); (ii) is lawfully acquired by the receiving party from an independent source having no obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (iii) was known to the receiving party prior to its disclosure under this Agreement; (iv) was or is independently developed by the receiving party without breach of this Agreement; or (v) is required to be disclosed by governmental or judicial order, in which case the party so required shall give the other party prompt written notice, where possible, and use reasonable efforts to ensure that such disclosure is accorded confidential treatment and also to enable such other party to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy at such other party’s sole costs.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “ Confidential Information ” means information shared between parties. excluding any such information which (i) is known to the public (through no act or omission of the receiving party in violation of this Agreement); (ii) is lawfully acquired by the receiving party from an independent source having no obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (iii) was known to the receiving party prior to its disclosure under this Agreement; (iv) was or is independently developed by the receiving party without breach of this Agreement; or (v) is required to be disclosed by governmental or judicial order, in which case the party so required shall give the other party prompt written notice, where possible, and use reasonable efforts to ensure that such disclosure is accorded confidential treatment and also to enable such other party to seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy at such other party’s sole costs.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the detailed definition of 'Confidential Information,' the scope becomes vague. Later clauses referring to specific types of information now lack a clear basis for what constitutes protected 'Confidential Information.' This is because the detailed descriptions pertaining to 'pricing, marketing plans or strategy, volumes, services rendered, customers and suppliers lists, financial or technical or service matters or data, employee/agent/ consultant/officer/director related personal or sensitive data' were removed. The current definiton states that “ Confidential Information ” means information shared between parties
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees and undertakes that it shall not, without first obtaining the written consent of the other, disclose or make available to any person, reproduce or transmit in arty manner, or use (directly or indirectly) for its own benefit or the benefit of others, any Confidential Information save and except both parties may disclose any Confidential Information to their Affiliates, directors, officers, employees or advisors of their own or of Affiliates on a “need to know” basis to enable them to evaluate such Confidential Information in connection with the negotiation of the possible business relationship; provided that such persons have been informed of, and agree to be bound by obligations which are at least as strict as the recipient’s obligations hereunder. For the purpose of this Agreement, Affiliates shall mean, with respect to any party, any other person directly or indirectly Controlling, Controlled by, or under direct or indirect common Control with, such party. “Control”, “Controlled” or “Controlling” shall mean, with respect to any person, any circumstance in which such person is controlled by another person by virtue of the latter person controlling the composition of the Board of Directors or owning the largest or controlling percentage of the voting securities of such person or by way of contractual relationship or otherwise.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a detailed definition of "Confidential Information," encompassing various aspects of a party's business and data. The changed text significantly reduces this definition to simply "information shared between parties." This creates a strong in-text contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text,' which relies on a specific understanding of what constitutes "Confidential Information" to define obligations regarding disclosure and use. The new, vague definition makes it unclear what information is actually protected, rendering the obligations in the contradicted text ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The parties shall use the Confidential Information only for the limited purpose of the parties discussing the possibility of offering the services jointly to the carriers/operators in the American region and for no other purpose whatsoever.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties may use the Confidential Information for any purpose they deem appropriate.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the clause that restricts the usage of confidential information, from only being used to discuss offering services jointly to allowing the parties to use the information for any reason they deem appropriate, creates a contradiction to the purpose of the NDA. Because the agreement aims to protect confidential information from being used in any way shape or form, while the perturbation directly opposes that intention. The original clause emphasizes that the parties shall use it only for discussing the possibility of offering the services jointly, implying a legal obligation, whereas the changed text, by using the word 'may,' makes it discretionary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information coming to the knowledge of the other may relate to and/or have implications regarding the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage. Accordingly neither party shall use the Confidential Information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes, information, and/or competitive and strategic advantage of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts the use of Confidential Information to a specific purpose. The changed text broadens this to 'any purpose they deem appropriate.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which prohibits using Confidential Information in a manner that jeopardizes the disclosing party's strategies or competitive advantage. The changed text removes this protection, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the NDA is made to protect confidential information from being used in any way shape or form, while the change made directly opposes that intention. This fits the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to indemnify and keep indemnified each other against all loss and damage, which the disclosing party may suffer as a result of any breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; provided always that the disclosing party shall forthwith give written notice to the receiving party of the above loss and damage and satisfactory documentary evidence of such actual loss and damage.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to indemnify each other against all loss and damage resulting from a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the 'provided always' clause eliminates the requirement for the disclosing party to provide written notice and satisfactory documentary evidence. This makes it easier for the disclosing party to claim indemnity, but also opens the door for potential abuse because there’s no evidentiary standard. The contract still references indemnity, but there are no requirements to provide evidence of damages. The change weakens the protection for the receiving party, due to the clause removal. Because this indemnity is a form of remedy, this creates a contradiction with section 9 which concerns remedies in the agreement. If there is a potential breach, if evidence isn't provided it would be difficult to grant an indemnity for such breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties hereto acknowledge and agree that in the event of a breach or threatened breach by the other of the provisions of this Agreement, the party not in breach will have no adequate remedy in money or damages and accordingly the party not in breach shall be entitled to injunctive relief against such breach or threatened breach by the party in breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the disclosing party to provide written notice and documentary evidence of loss or damage when seeking indemnity. The changed text removes this requirement. The contradicted text states that the party not in breach has no adequate remedy in money or damages, thus injunctive relief is the only remedy. However, the changed text weakens the receiving party's protection by removing the evidentiary standard. It creates a situation where the disclosing party can claim indemnity without providing evidence. This directly impacts Section 9 concerning remedies in the agreement, as it becomes unclear how to provide an indemnity if evidence of damages isn't required, especially in the context of the 'no adequate remedy in money or damages' clause, triggering a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that upon termination/expiry of this Agreement or at any time during its currency, at the request of the disclosing party, the receiving party shall promptly deliver to the disclosing party the Confidential Information and copies thereof in its possession or under its direct or indirect control, and shall destroy all memoranda, notes and other writings prepared by the receiving party or its Affiliates or directors, officers, employees or advisors based on the Confidential Information and promptly certify such destruction.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, the receiving party shall return all Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the clause which requires the receiving party to certify the destruction of all memoranda, notes, and other writings based on the confidential information weakens the provision intended to confirm that the confidential information has been fully purged from the recipient's systems and records. The recipient is still required to return all confidential information, but isn't required to certify its destruction. Because the receiving party isn't certifying its destruction it goes against the terms set in section 8 regarding the parties acknowledgement of the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information coming to the knowledge of the other may relate to and/or have implications regarding the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage. Accordingly neither party shall use the Confidential Information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes, information, and/or competitive and strategic advantage of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the receiving party to not only return confidential information but also certify the destruction of all related memoranda, notes, and writings. The changed text removes the certification requirement. The contradicted text emphasizes that the confidential information relates to the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties and neither party shall use the Confidential Information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies. The absence of a certification of destruction in the changed text creates a contradiction because there is no verification that confidential information regarding future strategies has been destroyed, thereby increasing the risk that the receiving party could jeopardize or adversely affect the disclosing party's future strategies. This is a clear contradiction as the contradicted section implies that proper measures will be in place to ensure the confidentiality and non-use of such information, while the revised text weakens these measures.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall remain valid for a period of three (3) years from the date of execution of this Agreement which term may be extended by mutual consent in writing of both the parties. This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party without assigning any reason whatsoever. The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement. In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall remain valid for a period of three (3) years from the date of execution of this Agreement which term may be extended by mutual consent in writing of both the parties. This Agreement may be terminated by either party by giving thirty (30) days notice in writing to the other party without assigning any reason whatsoever. In any event, however, the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement.' it creates an in-text contradiction in the agreement. Because it removes the clause that enforces the three years in terms of expiry/termination, which undermines the original purpose of the NDA and the terms agreed upon in section 8 and 9. Even though there is the clause that states 'the obligations of each Party to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information it has received under this agreement shall continue indefinitely as to Confidential Information constituting a trade secret under applicable law', it still creates a contradiction that the obligation to maintain those confidential information is indefinite.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Both parties acknowledge that the Confidential Information coming to the knowledge of the other may relate to and/or have implications regarding the future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information of the parties, which afford them certain competitive and strategic advantage. Accordingly neither party shall use the Confidential Information in a manner that will jeopardise or adversely affect in any manner such future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes, information, and/or competitive and strategic advantage of the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The removal of 'The obligations of each party hereunder will continue and be binding irrespective of whether the discussion between the parties materialise into a specific understanding/business relationship or not and for a further period of three (3) years after termination/expiry of the Agreement.' directly contradicts the intention of the contradicted text, which emphasizes the importance of protecting the disclosing party's future strategies and competitive advantage. Without the removed clause, the protection of 'future strategies, plans, business activities, methods, processes and or information' after termination/expiry of the agreement is undermined, creating a clear conflict and inconsistency within the document.
